Moses spoke to the people everything the Lord had given him as a command. Horeb was only eleven days distant from Kadesh-barnea. This was to remind them that their own bad behavior caused their long wanderings; so they might better understand the benefits of obedience. They must now go forward. Though God brings his people into trouble and suffering, he knows when they have been tried long enough. When God commands us to go forward in our Christian life, he sets heavenly Canaan before us for our encouragement.

Moses reminds the people of the happy structure of their government, which could make them all safe and secure if it was not their own fault. He acknowledges the fulfillment of God's promise to Abraham, and prays for its further accomplishment. We are not limited by the power and goodness of God; why should we be limited in our faith and hope? Good laws were given to the Israelites, and good men were to see that they were carried out, which showed God’s goodness to them, and the care of Moses.

Moses reminds the Israelites of their march from Horeb to Kadesh-barnea, through that great and terrible wilderness. He shows how close they were to a happy settlement in Canaan. It will increase the eternal ruin of hypocrites that they were not far from the kingdom of God. As if it were not enough that they had God before them, they would send men ahead of them. No one has looked into the Holy Land without acknowledging it to be a good land. And was there any reason to distrust this God? An unbelieving heart was at the root of all this. All disobedience to God’s laws and distrust of his power and goodness flow from disbelief of his word, as all true obedience springs from faith. It is helpful for us to divide our past lives into distinct periods; to give thanks to God for the mercies we have received in each, to confess and seek forgiveness for all the sins we can remember; and thus to renew our acceptance of God’s salvation, and our surrender of ourselves to his service. Our own plans seldom succeed; while courage in the exercise of faith, and in the path of duty, enables the believer to follow the Lord fully, to disregard all that opposes, to triumph over all opposition, and to take firm hold upon the promised blessings.
